ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 / Wicked Panda IIS web-shell post-exploitation (w3wp → recon LOLBin chain)
ucs.description: Hunts suspicious child processes spawned by an IIS / Tomcat / Nginx worker — the canonical signal of an ASPXSpy or China Chopper webshell being driven interactively. APT41 (BARIUM, Brass Typhoon) routinely chains T1190 exploitation of internet-facing apps (Citrix CVE-2019-19781, Zoho ManageEngine ADSelfService Plus CVE-2021-40539, Confluence, Log4Shell, USAHerds) into web-shell drop, then issues ad-hoc `whoami` / `net` / `nltest` / `certutil` / `bitsadmin` recon and stager pulls under the IIS application-pool identity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_hash) as sha256 from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","node.exe","nginx.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe") AND NOT (Processes.user="*$" OR Processes.user="NT AUTHORITY\\SYSTEM") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT41 webshell-driven recon under IIS / app-server worker process
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let _web_workers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","node.exe","nginx.exe","java.exe"]);
let _recon_lolbins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","hostname.exe","ping.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_web_workers)
| where FileName in~ (_recon_lolbins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend IsCertUtilDownload = (FileName =~ "certutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-urlcache","-split","-decode","-f http","-f https"))
| extend IsBitsTransfer = (FileName =~ "bitsadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/transfer")
| extend IsDomainRecon = (FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("group","localgroup","domain_trusts","dclist","user /domain","view"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ChildBin = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsCertUtilDownload, IsBitsTransfer, IsDomainRecon, SHA256
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            UniqueChildren = dcount(ChildBin),
            ChildBins = make_set(ChildBin, 25),
            CmdSamples = make_set(ChildCmd, 25),
            DownloadHits = countif(IsCertUtilDownload or IsBitsTransfer),
            ReconHits = countif(IsDomainRecon)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, ParentBin
| where UniqueChildren >= 2 or DownloadHits > 0 or ReconHits > 0
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 webshell-driven recon under IIS / app-server worker process — Sentinel SecurityEvent (4688)
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let _web_workers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","node.exe","nginx.exe","java.exe"]);
let _recon_lolbins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin in (_web_workers)
| where ChildBin in (_recon_lolbins)
| extend IsCertUtilDownload = (ChildBin == "certutil.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("-urlcache","-split","-decode","-f http","-f https"))
| extend IsBitsTransfer = (ChildBin == "bitsadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "/transfer")
| extend IsDomainRecon = (ChildBin in ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe") and CommandLine has_any ("group","localgroup","domain_trusts","dclist","user /domain","view"))
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            UniqueChildren = dcount(ChildBin),
            ChildBins = make_set(ChildBin, 25),
            CmdSamples = make_set(CommandLine, 25),
            DownloadHits = countif(IsCertUtilDownload or IsBitsTransfer),
            ReconHits = countif(IsDomainRecon)
            by Computer, Account, ParentBin
| where UniqueChildren >= 2 or DownloadHits > 0 or ReconHits > 0
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT41 Webshell Post-Exploitation via IIS / App-Server Worker Process
id: c4a7e132-8b5c-4f3d-a91e-apt41wsrecon01
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious recon / LOLBin child processes spawned by web-server worker processes (w3wp, tomcat, nginx, node) — the characteristic post-exploitation footprint of APT41 / BARIUM / Wicked Panda China Chopper / ASPXSpy webshells following T1190 exploitation of internet-facing apps.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0096/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-us-state-governments
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/apt41-initiates-global-intrusion-campaign-using-multiple-exploits
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.t1082
  - attack.g0096
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\node.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate diagnostic scripts run by web admins under the application-pool identity
  - Some monitoring tools invoke whoami / ipconfig from w3wp for healthchecks — baseline & allow-list
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT41 has a multi-year, well-documented playbook of compromising internet-facing application servers (Citrix NetScaler, Zoho ManageEngine, USAHerds, Confluence, Log4Shell, IIS via deserialization) and dropping ASPXSpy / China Chopper / custom .aspx web shells. Operator interaction with those shells produces an unmistakable telemetry signature: the web-server worker process — never normally a paren
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT41 staging chain: certutil/bitsadmin download → masqueraded scheduled-task persistence
ucs.description: Correlates an inbound payload pull via certutil / bitsadmin / PowerShell IWR with a scheduled-task creation on the SAME host within 30 minutes whose task name masquerades as a legitimate Microsoft component (T1036.004 / T1036.005). APT41 / Brass Typhoon repeatedly stages KEYPLUG, DUSTPAN, DUSTTRAP, ScrambleCross and CROSSWALK loaders this way and registers persistence with names like `\Microsoft\Windows\AppID\EPolicyManager`, `OneDriveUpdater`, `MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTask`, `WindowsDefenderUpdate` to blend into svchost / Microsoft scheduled-task tree.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as download_time values(Processes.process) as download_cmd values(Processes.process_name) as download_bin from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ((Processes.process_name=certutil.exe AND (Processes.process="*-urlcache*" OR Processes.process="*-split*" OR Processes.process="*-decode*" OR Processes.process="*-f http*")) OR (Processes.process_name=bitsadmin.exe AND Processes.process="*/transfer*") OR ((Processes.process_name=powershell.exe OR Processes.process_name=pwsh.exe) AND (Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*WebClient*"))) AND NOT Processes.user="*$" by host Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as task_time values(Processes.process) as task_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (schtasks.exe,at.exe) AND Processes.process="*/create*" AND (Processes.process="*Microsoft\\Windows\\*" OR Processes.process="*Update*" OR Processes.process="*Defender*" OR Processes.process="*Edge*" OR Processes.process="*OneDrive*" OR Processes.process="*NetSvc*" OR Processes.process="*Spooler*" OR Processes.process="*TimeBroker*" OR Processes.process="*WindowsBackup*") by host Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] | where task_time >= download_time AND task_time <= download_time+1800 | eval delay_sec = task_time - download_time | convert ctime(download_time) ctime(task_time) | table host user download_bin download_cmd task_cmd delay_sec download_time task_time | sort - task_time
ucs.kql:
// APT41 stage-then-persist: download → masqueraded scheduled task within 30 min on same host
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let downloads =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName =~ "certutil.exe"
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-urlcache","-split","-decode","-f http","-f https"))
         or (FileName =~ "bitsadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/transfer")
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","iwr ","Net.WebClient","Start-BitsTransfer"))
    | project DownloadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              DownloadBin = FileName, DownloadCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              DownloadParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
| where FileName in~ ("schtasks.exe","at.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","-Create","/run")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// APT41-style task-name masquerading — Microsoft / Windows / vendor-update mimicry
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/tn\s+\"?\\?(Microsoft\\Windows\\|Windows\\|.*Update|.*Defender|.*Security|.*NetSvc|.*Spooler|.*TimeBroker|.*Edge|.*OneDrive|.*Google|.*Adobe|.*WindowsBackup|.*EPolicyManager|.*AppID)"
| join kind=inner downloads on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DownloadTime .. DownloadTime + WindowMin)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, DownloadTime)
| project DownloadTime, TaskCreateTime = Timestamp, DelaySec,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          DownloadParent, DownloadBin, DownloadCmd,
          TaskBin = FileName, TaskCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          TaskParent = InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by TaskCreateTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT41 stage-then-persist on the same host within 30 min — Sentinel SecurityEvent (4688)
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let downloads =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where (ChildBin == "certutil.exe"
             and CommandLine has_any ("-urlcache","-split","-decode","-f http","-f https"))
         or (ChildBin == "bitsadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "/transfer")
         or (ChildBin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and CommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","iwr ","Net.WebClient","Start-BitsTransfer"))
    | project DownloadTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              DownloadBin = ChildBin, DownloadCmd = CommandLine,
              DownloadParent = ParentProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend TaskBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where TaskBin in ("schtasks.exe","at.exe")
| where CommandLine has_any ("/create","-Create","/run")
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/tn\s+\"?\\?(Microsoft\\Windows\\|Windows\\|.*Update|.*Defender|.*Security|.*NetSvc|.*Spooler|.*TimeBroker|.*Edge|.*OneDrive|.*Google|.*Adobe|.*WindowsBackup|.*EPolicyManager|.*AppID)"
| join kind=inner downloads on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (DownloadTime .. DownloadTime + WindowMin)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, DownloadTime)
| project DownloadTime, TaskCreateTime = TimeGenerated, DelaySec,
          Computer, Account,
          DownloadParent, DownloadBin, DownloadCmd,
          TaskBin, TaskCmd = CommandLine, TaskParent = ParentProcessName
| order by TaskCreateTime desc
ucs.rationale: Mandiant's 2022 (`Does This Look Infected?`), 2023 and 2024 reporting on APT41 — including the DUSTPAN / DUSTTRAP / KEYPLUG family — repeatedly highlights the same install pattern: a LOLBin (most often `certutil -urlcache -split -f`, `bitsadmin /transfer`, or PowerShell `Net.WebClient`) pulls down the loader, followed within seconds-to-minutes by a `schtasks /create` whose `/tn` value sits inside
